articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

article.headline: Mbappé on target as France thrash Scotland in friendly
article.description: France recovered from a glaring defensive error to beat Scotland 4-1 in a friendly between two teams that have already qualified for the European Championship.
article.linkText: Mbappé on target as France thrash Scotland

images.name: France celeb vs. Scotland_20231017 [1296x729]
images.caption: Benjamin Pavard celebrates with Kylian Mbappe after scoring France's second goal against Scotland.
images.alt: France's Benjamin Pavard celebrates with Kylian Mbappe after making it 2-1  during an international friendly match between France and Scotland at Stade Pierre Mauroy, on October 17, 2023, in Lille, France.
images.credit: Craig Foy/SNS Group via Getty Images

article.story:
France recovered from a glaring defensive error to beat Scotland 4-1 in a friendly between two teams that have already qualified for the European Championship.

Kickoff in Lille was delayed by 10 minutes to allow fans time to get safely into the ground, due to enhanced security measures in place.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

Scotland led briefly after an errant pass from Eduardo Camavinga allowed Billy Gilmour to score the opening goal, but Benjamin Pavard equalised five minutes later and scored a brace before half-time.

Kylian Mbappé converted a penalty for France just before the break, after Liam Cooper was penalised for a shirt tug on Olivier Giroud and Kingsley Coman completed the scoring when he netted a rebound in the 70th minute.

Both teams secured their places at next year's Euros in Germany in the last few days, ahead of the final round of qualifiers next month. France will round out its group with a visit from Gibraltar and a trip to Greece, while Scotland will travel to Georgia and host Norway

Reuters and the Associated Press contributed to this report.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Goal! France 0, Scotland 1. Billy Gilmour (Scotland) right footed shot from the left side of the box to the bottom right corner.
Goal! France 1, Scotland 1. Benjamin Pavard (France) header from the left side of the six yard box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Antoine Griezmann with a cross following a corner.
Goal! France 2, Scotland 1. Benjamin Pavard (France) header from very close range to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Kylian Mbappé following a set piece situation.
Nathan Patterson (Scotland) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Liam Cooper (Scotland) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal! France 3, Scotland 1. Kylian Mbappé (France) converts the penalty with a right footed shot to the top right corner.
Substitution, Scotland. Zander Clark replaces Liam Kelly.
Substitution, France. Marcus Thuram replaces Olivier Giroud.
Substitution, France. Kingsley Coman replaces Ousmane Dembélé.
Substitution, Scotland. John Souttar replaces Liam Cooper.
Substitution, Scotland. Jacob Brown replaces Ché Adams.
Goal! France 4, Scotland 1. Kingsley Coman (France) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the high centre of the goal.
Substitution, France. Youssouf Fofana replaces Antoine Griezmann.
Substitution, France. Boubacar Kamara replaces Aurélien Tchouaméni.
Substitution, Scotland. Stuart Armstrong replaces Billy Gilmour.
Substitution, Scotland. Ryan Christie replaces Kenny McLean.
Greg Taylor (Scotland) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, France. Castello Lukeba replaces Ibrahima Konaté.
Substitution, France. Randal Kolo Muani replaces Kylian Mbappé.
Substitution, Scotland. John McGinn replaces Nathan Patterson.
Second Half ends, France 4, Scotland 1.
